howItWorks.intro: This estate tax calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Taxable Estate
rules.formula: Taxable Estate = Gross Estate - Debts - Charitable Gifts - Marital Deduction
rules.explanation: The estate value subject to tax after all deductions.

rules.name: Estate Tax
rules.formula: Progressive rates from 18% to 40% on amount above exemption
rules.explanation: Federal estate tax applies progressive rates to the amount exceeding the exemption.

howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is the estate tax exemption for 2024?
faq.answer: The federal estate tax exemption is $13.61 million per individual ($27.22 million for married couples) in 2024. Estates below this threshold owe no federal estate tax. This amount is set to drop to roughly $7 million in 2026 unless Congress acts.

faq.question: What is the marital deduction?
faq.answer: Assets left to a surviving US citizen spouse are fully exempt from estate tax through the unlimited marital deduction. This defers tax until the surviving spouse's death, at which point their combined estate may be taxed.

faq.question: Is the Estate Tax Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard financial planning formulas trusted by individuals and families. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What gross estate value should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate gross estate value value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Estate Tax Calculator?
sections.body: The Estate Tax Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your gross estate value, debts and expenses, charitable bequests, you get instant results including taxable estate, federal exemption, amount above exemption. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting taxable estate right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your gross estate value and need to find the right taxable estate. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Gross Estate Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your gross estate value — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: debts and expenses, charitable bequests, marital deduction. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your taxable estate, federal exemption, amount above exemption. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your gross estate value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both taxable estate and federal exemption — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your marital deduction, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
